evals.prompt: My game looks technically fine but plays badly — players keep missing enemies and important objects, and the screen feels busy. Separately, I have only a vague art idea and need to brief an artist. Help with both.
evals.expected_output: Applies the visual information-channels idea (color/shape/surface-contrast; removing channels hurts playability), addresses composition (maximize actionable play-area, separate foreground from background, deliberate camera), and gives a breadth-first reference-gathering plus artist-briefing workflow with concrete refs and rough programmer-art.
expectations: Uses the visual information-channels idea (color/shape/surface-contrast; removing channels like via monochrome or uniform roughness hurts playability, not just looks)
Addresses composition: maximize actionable play-area, separate foreground from background, camera as a deliberate choice
Gives a reference-gathering + artist-briefing workflow (low-bar/breadth-first references, brief with concrete refs plus a rough programmer-art sketch)
